I canceled my phone service and Apple Watch service with them back in July. I transferred my phone service over to another provider and everything was supposed to have ended at that point contract wise as I had paid for all my devices in full. Unfortunately, they kept billing me for the Apple Watch service because they never canceled that, only the phone. I was automatically charged $11.41 on July 3rd and $15.00 on August 4th. This feels like a way for a multi billion dollar company to deceive people out of their money by billing them for a service they can’t even use. The phone and watch have to sync and being I transferred my phone service and canceled the Apple Watch or do I thought it was a useless service but yet they still just automatically took the money anyway. I contacted them today both by phone and in store and though I was able to cancel it as of today they refused to refund the prior two payments they already took for a service I couldn’t even use and  had canceled months prior. They claim I only transferred the phone and not the watch according to their notes and it doesn’t matter if I was paying for a useless service for the watch since of course t-mobile isn’t going to sync with spectrum phone service, they still refused to refund the $26.41. This seems like way for them to steal from unsuspecting customers who did what they were supposed to do as for cancellation goes and keep charging them for something small in comparison to what was being paid prior to cancellation and then refuse to refund charges that were supposed to have ended. I believe, this could be happening to hundreds or thousands of people or more and generating them millions in extra revenue for a service they aren’t truly providing. On top of that, when I signed up for the service I was deceived by the in store rep who told me and showed me false numbers saying my bill would be $98 a month for everything. It stuck my printed paperwork (I signed everything on a tablet in store) under my new boxes in the bottom of a bag and did so by taking advantage of the fact that I had two young restless kids with me. So not only did I get lied to and stuck in a over a two year contract that cost me and extra $80 a month over my intended budget and what I was promised, but after all of that they steal $26.41 after I cancelled and said it doesn’t matter I couldn’t connect the watch to use what I was paying for, doesn’t matter I cancelled my phone service and transferred that number, doesn’t matter that I’m absolutely certain I wanted everything cancelled because why would I keep service on a watch I can’t use….they absolutely will not refund the money.. That is theft by deception, fraud, lies on their part and just wrong!!!

Seguiré tratando de desasearme de estos estafadores, no caigan en sus promociones que no existen.

Title: Misled, Overcharged, and Deceived – Bait and switch tactics - Buyer Beware

We were promised by a T-Mobile representative that if we switched from AT&T, we would receive free phones, promotional pricing, and an Apple employee discount. 

Our quoted monthly bill (before taxes) was $157. Instead, our actual bills have been $579 per month — more than $320 higher than our reliable AT&T service of 25 years.

Not a single promise has been honored. We are being charged for phones that were supposed to be free, the promotional pricing never appeared, and the Apple employee discount was ignored. To make matters worse, we constantly see T-Mobile advertising these same promotions in commercials — the very ones that were used to lure us in — which makes the deception even more frustrating.

We’ve had over a dozen interactions with customer service. Each time, a different representative gave us a slightly different explanation and resolution. One even changed our plan without our permission. Another representative admitted the errors were on T-Mobile’s side and removed autopay to prevent erroneous charges, but no corrections were ever made and promised follow-up calls never happened.

This has been an exhausting, frustrating, and dishonest experience from start to finish. Instead of saving money, we are paying hundreds more and wasting countless hours trying to get answers.

Bottom line: T-Mobile’s sales tactics feel deceptive, their commercials continue to advertise promotions that were not honored, and their customer support is inconsistent at best. We regret ever switching and warn others: do not trust their sales pitch.

I am a metro pcs customer and was taking advantage of an offer to get a free phone if I switch to a T-Mobile plan. I was not allowed to do so even though it was still being advertised in the store and on the website. The metro customer service representative in the store informed me that he just signed another customer up for the exact same deal he was attempting to sign me up for just a little over an hour prior to assisting me. The T-Mobile customer service representative was cordial but for some reason, no longer had access to the deal on his end . In addition the process took well over an hour and we were disconnected multiple times.

Please know that if you choose one of T Mobile discounted plans like the over 55 plan, the free phone on us does not apply. You can’t get a discounted plan and a free phone and they will hide that using ambiguous language. However, they would absolutely prefer you get a free phone on a permanently non discounted plan than for you to be on a permanently discounted plan because eventually you’ll be paying more than the cost of the phone.

I’ve been a T-Mobile customer for years, but I’m shocked at how their policies are keeping me stuck in a situation I can’t control. I’m divorced, and the family phone plan is still under my ex-husband’s name. I’ve tried to move my line to another carrier so I can manage my own account, but T-Mobile refuses to let me without his permission.

This means my ex has complete control over whether I can keep or transfer my own phone number. After 15 years of marriage, and despite having zero history of harming or misusing accounts, I’m treated like I can’t be trusted with my own service. It feels outdated, unfair, and frankly unsafe to give one person that much power over another’s independence.

All I want is the freedom to manage my own phone line — without being at the mercy of someone else’s choices. T-Mobile, it’s 2025. There needs to be a better way for divorced or separated customers to reclaim their accounts and numbers. Until this changes, I can’t recommend T-Mobile to anyone in similar circumstances.

I switched to TMobile in March of 2025, because I hated how difficult is was to reach Comcast Customer Service, my previous Mobile provider, and because they boast the best 5G network in USA.
Reaching TMobile customer service is very easy so I was extremely pleased with this, however my wireless service leaves much to be desired.  It seems to work great when I am away from home, but my service at home, in the west mesa of Albuquerque is terrible.  Each time I speak with their customer service reps, who are always friendly and eager to help, they never seem to be able to resolve my issue, which I believe is a lack of cell towers in my area.  That said, I don't have the coverage I need where I need it most.  Last week my Comcast internet went down for a day and TMobile simply wasn't able to pick up the slack.  Without my comcast internet, I was unable to reach the internet on my mobile, however I was able to make calls, only.  From day one I have also had trouble sending text messages. Sixty to Seventy percent of the time I get a Message not sent error/Resend or Delete message.  That said, I feel like I haven't gotten what I pay for the last 5 or 6 months, since I switched.  Furthermore I rarely get a 5G connection from home, and when I do it is short lived, so when I see the best 5G commercials in the USA on TV, it really frustrates me.

I was told T-mobile would be less than what I was paying for Verizon so I changed over and got a new phone. Wellllll, the hassle to get the phone was crazy, should have went to the store to do it all. Then I realized they didn’t send the watch we discussed, my fault for not realizing that. Then the process was crazy. Then I found out that I was going to be paying about as much as I was with Verizon. Then when trying to switch my number back they told me they couldn’t get me a transfer number. The rep from Verizon asked why? I told him I was literally on the device that is connected to the account. This has been the most horrible experience I have ever had transferring a number to another service. T-mobile did nothing to make this easy. And no one communicates with anyone else because different people tell me different things. Very frustrating

Now they’re saying I have to wait 48 hours for someone to “find” where the money went. Have the charge listed on my account for proof for then and they couldn’t care less. Just kept saying “try not to worry” which is easy to say when it’s not their money being stolen!

What does it take to put up a few panels facing all points of the compass

T-Mobile owes us almost $1,000 and we cannot get anyone in management to call us back. NEVER make a cash payment to this company!

We'll end our 22 year relationship over this breach of trust.

We wanted to get Internet service for an elderly family member. He couldn't come to the store, so I went and explained the situation. They asked for my ID. I gave it to them, and said, look, I just want to see if he can get service. Is he in your coverage area? They copied my ID then asked for my social security number. I said I wasn't comfortable giving that until I knew that he could get service. They said there was no way that they could check to see if he could get service unless I gave them my social security number—even though he doesn't live with me and the service would be at a different address than mine. That's such bull. Anyway, I walked out. We'll pay more for Verizon, but at least they don't ask for personal information before letting you know if you're in their coverage area.

Successfully explaining the reason for calling not one of the agents were able to repeat and understand what I said. after the fourth time of explaining to no avail, becoming extremely distressed mentally and physically, I had to end the call without obtaining the information sought.

If I could give T-Mobile zero stars, I would. This has been the single worst experience I’ve had with any service provider — internet, phone, or otherwise. It started with what seemed like a simple sign-up for their home internet service. That was my first mistake.
The Internet Never Worked
From the moment I signed up, the internet never actually functioned. Not slow. Not spotty. Just did not work. I had to rely on a public library hotspot just to get basic online tasks done — in 2025. Absolutely ridiculous.

Website Failures and Billing Traps
Their website is laughably unreliable. I tried to set up auto-payment — it cut out in the middle of the process and didn’t save my information. No warning. No notification. Just a surprise suspension of service. When I called to resolve it, they hit me with a $70 reconnection fee and a $7.77 late fee. All this after they failed to process the payment due to their broken site.
Refused to Cancel Promptly
When I decided I’d had enough and wanted to cancel, they told me I’d have to wait until the next billing cycle. Let me remind you: the internet never worked in the first place. It wasn’t until I threatened a chargeback that they agreed to cancel on the spot. So apparently they can cancel right away — they just choose not to unless you push hard enough.

Still Waiting on a Refund — 6 Months Later
And here’s the kicker: six months later, I am still owed a refund. T-Mobile did not automatically process the return of my money like every other halfway-competent company on Earth. I had to call half a dozen times, each time being promised a different resolution. I even went into a physical store — guess what? They couldn’t help either. "It’s out of our hands." Of course it is.

Final Thoughts
This company is either massively understaffed, completely disorganized, or simply doesn’t care. Possibly all three. They’re quick to charge you for services they never deliver and maddeningly slow — or outright unwilling — to give you back your money when they fail. Customer service is a rotating door of empty apologies and non-answers.
If you're thinking about signing up for T-Mobile's internet service, don't walk — RUN in the opposite direction. The frustration and wasted time are not worth it. I’ve never experienced a company so unprofessional and apathetic toward its customers. 

Oh and when I called to get help fixing the internet, they told me to use their diagnostic app — which, unbelievably, requires an internet connection to function. Let that sink in: I couldn’t fix the internet because I didn’t have internet to run the tool to fix the internet. It would be laughable if it weren’t so maddening.

lol I was also asked when I cancelled if “there was anyone else in my home who wanted to take over the service”

I was excited to try T-Satellite as a non-T-Mobile customer. I was sold a FALSE bill of goods. 
Background: I have a compatible unlocked iPhone from another carrier. 
I was TOLD by the sales person I would have text, voice and data capabilities. THAT WAS A LIE. They only support text by satellite for iPhones as of Aug 27th. Only consider if you have exceptionally strong reasons to stay in contact. 
TRUTH: 
- $10 Activation; 
- 30-day trial; 
- Massive sign-up hassle (incl. credit checks; side note: their anti-fraud/corporate security is higher than most banks I've used-- for $10 a month--seriously?); 
- $10 or $15 per month thereafter (differing info from sales and tech support)
- maybe, possibly, someday they will offer voice and data to non-T-Mobile customers paying for T-Satellite. 

PERFORMANCE:
- TECHNICAL CHALLENGES. For T-Satellite to work most effectively, I have to turn off my primary line. This causes all kinds of other issues and hassles to manage related to your Apple accounts - iMessage, etc. 
- CONNECTIVITY - Once the primary line is turned off T-Satellite connectivity was very good and consistent. I'd say it held the satellite connection 98% of the time. Texts went through quickly (via SMS).

One problem is over a period of time i have been been getting error messages back from people I text to which read: free msg: Unable to send message-message blocking is active. When I contacted their so called technical support today a person said that I must have forgotten I added message block in June. I said thats a lie and fix it and dont tell me that.

They sent me a phone without the S Pen. Then when it didn't work they said call Samsung.  I have talked to 30 (not exaggerating) customer service people. One tried to trick me into having two phone payments for the same line. This company is terrible and I miss Sprint. You can't pay for your phones early anymore which they neglected to mention.

They have no authority to do anything they have to send request to get things done. It is a continual nightmare to get anything accomplished. So happy I left.

T-Mobile has regressed into what it was before the days of John Legere.  We received an email for a "free" phone.  My wife needed one so we went into the store and was told that her phone qualified for a trade in.  Bottom line the sales rep pushed several accessories on us saying they were free with the upgrade.  It turns out they weren't and neither was the upgrade completely free.  We tried to return the items but were told we could since they had been used.  When I reminded the rep he told us the accessories were free his response was "I meant you didn't have to pay for them that day, so they were free that day."

Calling customer service didn't help either.  I'm told the new CEO puts a lot of pressure on store reps to sell accessories so they lie.  This problem isn't limited to my local store as the internet forums are full of similar tales.  

What I meant when I said T-Mobile had regressed into what it used to be was that before Legere this type of behavior from their sales reps were the norm.  They had a terrible reputation and were known as T-Horrible partly because of the scams their sales people pulled and partly because of their spotty coverage and poor customer service.  They got better under Legere but now that he's gone they've returned to what their old ways.

I called 5 times to unlock my prepaid phone. All of the representative said I am eligible to unlock my phone and my phone will unlock within 1 week but it never unlocked. Poor service, unreliable and so disappointing. I regret that I bought T-Mobile service. Lowest rating.

I racked up a $266.34 bill for a line in which I never used and for which the companies data services where never utilized. BEWARE. Also, one representative actually said that T-Mobile will NEVER reimburse to people who are no longer customers and that I would have to STAY a customer in order to even get a reimbursement if they decided I qualified for reimbursement.

Total scam. I went into the store for a yearly upgrade of my elderly parents' phone and mine. I control our shared account. The manager, without my permission, added several SIM cards to our account, each with a monthly charge, and fraudulently activated my parents' phones as new lines, causing a $100+ increase in the monthly bill. We learned later that the manager, who was later supposedly transferred to another store like an abusive Catholic priest, received bonuses for these added lines. We're now unable to back out of the extra charges because we'd have to pay full price for my parents' phones, over $800 a piece. T Mobile has offered no way to rectify this, instead blaming the manager who's no longer employed in the store. I've disputed the bill and T Mobile has responded by cutting service.

Customer service sucks....all they do is apologize but can't do anything to help!  DON'T SWITCH!!!!!!

T-Mobile customer service will only be nice to customers who do surveys. As long as you have a survey history, especially if you give 10/10, they'll give you almost anything you ask for. They're more likely to compensate you with credits too. T-Mobile phone deals are a a rip-off unless it's a trade-in. If the deal requires a new line, you end up paying more than if you just get the phone at a regular price. It's only with a trade-in deal that you actually get a bit of discount. They make you believe you're saving money by getting a device for let's say as an example, 10 dollars a month. But then you have to pay for the new line which let's say costs 25 dollars plus taxes. If you add it all up and compare it with the phone's regular price, you'll see it either costs more than or is exactly the same as the regular price. The discount is just an illusion. If you're on grandfathered plans where your lines cost 10-15 bucks a month then yes, you will get a discount on the phone. But if your plan is 25 bucks plus taxes for every new line, you're better off just getting the phone regular price. And again, you need to answer surveys and give them 10/10. You'll notice they'll treat you better and will be more likely to give you credits regardless of your tenurity with the company.

Absolutely horrible customer service. My Iphone was stolen and I was filing a claim. I went to a store where the lady working there gave me the wrong insurance company to call - she mentioned an old one. I found the correct one online. It took over a week to get my new phone. And when I called Customer Service, the man would not help me because he kept insisting on sending  a verification code to the stolen phone. He was very short and rude, as well. He actually said, "I have given you options already", when he actually had not given me any options.

Even after my new phone finally came in, the ESIM card is not activated, so I had to call Customer Service again, or reach them by the TMOBILE APP (as per what the instructions say to do). So, I couldn't call since the ESIM was not activated. I went to the website to get help. And that customer service agent verified my PII and confirmed my pin code. 

Although my identity was already confirmed, he still insisted on sending a verification code to the phone. The phone cannot get the code since the ESIM is not activated (and this is what I was calling for help with), and I asked if the verification  code can be emailed to me at the email on file, and he said they cannot. Basically, there is no recourse for help other than to send verification codes to devices that you cannot access, and so they refuse to help and tell you to go to the store (which was already closed). 

So, if you have your phone stolen, the Customer Service department is useless to help you because they are like robots that only want to send verification codes to the stolen phone. It is absolutely ridiculous. I regret paying for the replacement phone deductible of $249 now. I should have just gone with another company.

T-Mobile has given me THE WORST EXPIERIENCE on the phone. So I was looking to get a new phone and being a costumer of Metro by T-Mobile I went to the Metro store to see what if anything they can do to help me out. The agent at the Metro store wanted to sell me an IPhone 14 for over $600 and I told her that was too much for me right now. She asked "well, how much $$ do you have?" No thanks. I left and the ran out to the parking lot and said "come in and we'll help you out". I go in and they give me a promotion for a FREE IPhone 14, Samaung Tab and I can keep my same number I just had to pay $80  they just have to send it to my house and when I get it to  go to T-Mobile and activate it. FANTASTIC!!! So now to try to make this story short, UPS "couldn't find my house and sent it back to  T-Mobile" and have to call T-Mobile to have them send it out again. I was on the phone with T-Mobile "Costumer Service" for 1 hour & 37 min. only to have them hang up on me. I call back and by this time I don't even want the phone or tab I just want the $80 back that I paid and they "said" that they would refund but couldn't give me and email or text or anything saying that I have been refunded so I am going to assume that I'm out $80 and still have a messed up phone and my blood pressure is through the roof. Horrible, Unprofessional and I feel  violated after talking and dealing with T-Mobile.

I am a brand new T-Mobile customer who retired and then transferred all my lines from Verizon (23 year customer) to T-Mobile for cost savings.  Got a deal on the new Samsung Galaxy Fold 7 and was told at the store that I would receive it within 9 or 10 days and would receive an email confirming dates.  I was pleased because of a trip planned in 11 days and was  looking forward to having the new phone while traveling.  But when I received the email, it provided me different dates, the delivery was expected to be 12 to 18 days, not 9 or 10 as the store told me.  Following this email, there was no further communication from T-Mobile and so when 18 days passed I called looking for an update.

Well this is when T-Mobile totally disappointed me.  They told me the Fold 7 was back-ordered and that they could not provide any new date on which I might actually receive it.  The most current information they had was, "IT WAS BACK-ORDERED" and they had no additional information.  When I asked them to reset my expectation if I might receive it in a day, a week, a month or a year; they told me they could not provide any possible date.

Wow!  What a disappointment and such poor communication.  Surely a supervisor could provide a better response.  

Wow! I got more disappointment from the supervisor, who explained when new phones are released, they often become back-ordered and T-Mobile has been handling this "no communication" route the same for many years now.  What will happen once the original delivery date is missed, is nothing!  They do not send out any updated delivery date information, you must simply wait for a text message or email telling you that they have boxed up your new phone and created a tracking number and then you will get your phone in about four days after it is boxed.  But when my new phone would be boxed up was anybody's guess.  Until it's boxed, there will be no additional communication provided to me, no attempt to reset my expectations and no new target delivery date provided.  The supervisor did say when I pushed hard, that maybe I would get my phone in two weeks....maybe.

So as a brand new T-Mobile customer, I am TOTALLY DISAPPOINTED with T-Mobile's approach at communicating with new customers... i.e. just ignoring them!

What an amazingly poor impression T-Mobile has given me of how highly they value customers?  

Retired and disappointed in T-Mobile.

What deceitful practices! I had service with this company and left because the service is so poor. I left by just signing up with another company.  I also had a watch on my account.  I connected it to another account with a new number, just like my phone, but do you know what t-mobile did? they kept charging me for the watch line, even though it is active on another carrier.  I called them to complain of this and they said that even the watch is not even connected to t-mobile, they are still going to charge me because I did not call them to cancel the service. Yet, when I switched the phone at the same time, that plan was automatically canceled.  In my opinion, t-mobile is a dishonest company and lies to it's customers (me) in order to keep collecting money.  I do not recommend T-Mobile.  They have such horrible service in every area of the country that I visited while with their service.

I have been a loyal T-Mobile customer for over 17 years, but my recent experience has been
unacceptable and in violation of the FCC’s cell phone unlocking policy.
On Thursday, I called T-Mobile to confirm my account balance and inquire about transferring my
service to another carrier, which is also a subsidiary of T-Mobile. I was told about port transfers but not informed that my device needed to be unlocked. My phone has been fully paid off since April or May 2025.
After porting my number to the new carrier, I discovered I could not activate my phone because it was still locked to T-Mobile. The new carrier informed me of the lock, and I immediately contacted T-Mobile that same night. I was told an unlock request had been escalated and would be completed within 24‒72 hours.
Since then, I have called multiple times, had multiple unlock requests “escalated,” and still have
received no resolution and no communication from T-Mobile. It has now been over a week, and I am
paying for service with two carriers while being able to use my phone only on Wi-Fi.
Adding to the frustration, I visited the T-Mobile store in Germantown, MD for assistance. The store
manager showed no interest in helping me, stating she “can’t help customers” with this issue and
that I should call customer service. When I explained that I could not use my phone to make the call, she refused to assist, saying she has the same customer service number I do. This dismissive and unhelpful approach is unacceptable.
FCC policy requires carriers to unlock a paid-off device within two business days of receiving a
request. T-Mobile has failed to meet this standard despite multiple escalations. I am formally requesting immediate action to unlock my device and restore my ability to use the service I am paying for. If this matter is not resolved promptly, I will be filing a complaint with the FCC and considering additional actions to recover the costs I have incurred due to T-Mobile’s failure to act.
I expect confirmation that my phone is unlocked within 24 hours of this letter.

If you dont have Tmobile STOP! Run the other way, they absolutely suck! This is the ONLY review you will ever need to refer to. I could give you example after example that would make your head spin with surprised reactions, and it always boils down to the same thing...they either are complete morons and have no clue what they are doing and run the company poorly or they know exactly what they are doing, smart as a whip, but are lazy, useless people and they run the company into the ground. Take your pick...inconsiderate or incompetent...but in the end useless. But TM is the ONLY company i have used that sucks on a day to day basis...every single day there are dropped calls, poor reception, people wont be able to hear you, missed calls that wont show up on your phone, so you wont know you even had a missed call, even phones showing you are not connected to a network and can only make 911 calls; that one is my favorite by the way. If you owned this cess pool of a company and 85% of the people using your product said you suck, your lazy and your incompetent, wouldnt you want to do something to change this fact?!! Yes you do...so you morons cant get the cellphone correct, and you think a good offer is to ask if i want something else you can screw up every month? Really...when i turned down these gifts you know what they offered in place of these gifts...NOTHING!!!! So what they told meis that you dont care, which is why i am having so many issues, you only offer gifts that relate to you crooks making more money while keeping your incompetent status, and when i turn that down for obvious reasons, your not prepared to ffer anything else, because in the end you really dont care about customers!!!!!!!!! I usually don’t take time to write reviews but this t-mobile scam made to write this. I wanted to add a line to my plan. T-Mobile customer service told me the monthly fee will be $135 including 2 lines and the monthly payment for the phone I purchased from them if I trade in my old iPhone. They also added a third line which I was told it’s for free and up to me to use it or not. It is not going to affect my monthly payments. Then I realized they are billing me a family plan of $170,91 which is outrages. Since then I have been calling them spending hours first to be connected then being on hold for hours. But no one wanted to solve my issue. Long story short I am thinking of changing to a different provider.

I became a customer on June 13th. I was very happy that day. Then I got my first bill which was $67.00 more than promised. I went to a T-mobile store where I found out a promotion had not been applied to one of the phone which accounted for 49.00 of the overcharge. I also found out I was being charged a 4th line I did not agree to. I was overcharged again today because T-mobile needs 2 or more billing cycles to fix an overcharge of their doing and I will not receive money - which is mine - but instead credit for T-mobile. T-MOBILE's misleading practices guarantee that customers fall behind on their payments, enabling them to impose late fees that amount to millions of dollars a month in additional indirect profits for the company. They removed the late fee but declined to enroll me in autopay. I am an elderly senior in my 70s and lack internet access and do not have a Facebook account to post this on social media. I have been contacting T-Mobile at times with my friend's help from approximately July 2, 2025, until today, July 29, 2025, to settle the bill and enroll in autopay without incurring the additional $5.00 fee for my upcoming August 4, 2025, payment. However, they have consistently rejected my request for autopay, ensuring that I default, which will result in an extra $5.00 being added to my upcoming bill due on August 4th. All of this originated from T-Mobile disabling my ability to send and respond to text messages. Although my phone indicated that a text was sent, the messages were not actually delivered; it was a deceptive notification that led me to believe my text had been sent. By the time I realized T-Mobile's misrepresentations, three months had passed (from December to March). The total average text message count for each month was only 15 to 20 messages, but after opening a so-called ticket, T-Mobile would allow me to send texts for one day, claiming the issue was resolved, when in reality it was another scam, as they would subsequently revoke my ability to text or reply. I would receive notifications indicating sending and sent, even when nothing was actually transmitted. This situation persisted for six months, forcing me to demonstrate that I was not spamming. They had labeled me as a criminal for allegedly spamming against the law. Ultimately, I contacted the FCC, which swiftly acknowledged the problem as being associated with the T-Mobile carrier. I was astonished at how quickly the FCC representative could pinpoint T-Mobile as the mobile carrier, explaining that they had received a multitude of complaints regarding the company falsely accusing customers of spamming. On Thursday, April 3, 2025, the representative provided me with the government's definitions of spam and mass texting, along with the relevant rules and laws, assuring me that my 15-20 text messages per month did not amount to spamming, nor did the messages I had sent or received from others. They provided me an email with the following message: "We assigned your call Ticket No. 7758097. Here is a link to a consumer guide regarding your call - fcc.gov, consumers, guides,stop-unwanted-robocalls-and-texts. When I reported my discussion with T-Mobile, their technical department insisted that I disclose my text messages, as well as the names and phone numbers of those I had texted, as a condition for assisting me in resolving the issue. Once the FCC clarified that I was not engaging in any criminal activity, I felt relieved, as they had previously instilled fear in me that I had done something against the law. This fear was the reason for my calling to the FCC to assert my innocence, as I was unaware that I had violated any laws. I subsequently informed T-Mobile of their unfounded claims regarding my conduct, and only then did T-Mobile reveal that, while I was blocked from sending text messages, an unidentified individual was sending between 2000 and 3000 text messages per month using my number. When I pointed out that this occurred during the period they had restricted my texting capabilities, it became evident that they allowed others to somehow breach and access T-Mobile's database and misuse my number. The entire situation spiraled into chaos, prompting me to request adjustments; however, instead of addressing the matter thoroughly, they made absolutely no changes and to this date someone else is still texting over 2000 text messages to other using my T-Mobile phone number. The few dollars in credit they gave me represents only minor tweaks compared to the pain and suffering they had put me through. T-Mobiles arrogant attitude is as if they were doing me a favor, adjusting my account for $10 a month! This resulted in late charges, and concurrently, I lost my autopay feature. The entire ordeal has been a struggle since December 2024. This company operates as a monopoly after acquiring Sprint and several other companies like MetroPCS, thereby squeezing consumers. Furthermore, when I signed up with T-Mobile in February 2022, they assured me that I would be on a $40 plan with a lifetime price guarantee, which, as many are aware, is not the case.

After being with T-Mobile for over 15 years, I would not recommend them. 
I got one of their promotions to open another line and get a free phone. 
The line I opened was for my teen. I had to also pay monthly for family mode, so I could shut off certain websites etc, to keep her protected. 
She was able to hack around it and get in to it. 
So I called and canceled her line
I was then responsible for paying for my "free phone" which, in a normal circumstance, I would totally understand. But it's not my fault she was able to hack into the monitoring system that I paid monthly for. 
I called and spoke to a representative as well as a supervisor and neither of them would help me out and comp my phone given the circumstances.
Incredibly infuriating

I absolutely hate everything this company stands for. They are fraudulent scum bag conartists. They took advantage of me and my mother, this isn’t a joke. I seriously cannot put into words how much I hate this company.

I'm sorry to hear about all these one star experiences. They clearly didn't go to the right store and more importantly, they did not get to work with Damian. He is the poster boy for customer service.  I just switched to T-Mobile from ATT last week and had major issues with ATT.  Damian was kind, empathetic and friendly all while actually solving my problems.  Very subtle. I was done in 20 minutes! Thanks, Damian!

After being over 10 years with T-Mobile and having no issue with customer service it’s been horrible the last few times I have had to troubleshoot issues and billing the past year From messing up my bill /messing up the promotions after  I reverted everything back they still manager to reinstate a sync up drive, te me the device is no longer compatible but someone still requiring me to pay for the end of the month without even being able to use it. Least you could have done is credit the unused service or cancel it on the spot not saying needs to run until the end of the monthly dates. Best way to get rid of loyal customer. Disappointment.

If you have any alternative to T-Mobile Internet service where you live, I'd encourage you to use it because their customer service is so terrible that not only do they not help, they waste a bunch of your time on the way. 

I ordered this morning, and I immediately went in and started setting my profile and my "free" streaming services I get with it.  The site kept asking for a T-Mobile number, so I tried their chatbot. That's useless (their AI is worse than usual, which is not ideal for a tech company). I manage to get a hold of a person to chat with, and they take some time to figure out that they can't do that, and I'll have to call in. They also can't just cancel the order via chat, even immediately after, so I call in to cancel because if I'm already having problems at this point, this is a bad idea. I call. After waiting on hold, I speak to someone who cancels it and promises me I'll get an email in a few minutes. When I haven't gotten an email after about 20, I try to check and see if it's canceled. I can't find that out without talking to a chat agent again, so I do that, and she can't tell me if it's canceled, nor can she assist me in any other way. I should just wait and let them deliver the expensive equipment to my house so I can be responsible for getting it back to them. I call again to speak to someone, who tells me they can't cancel it via telephone, so I ask for a supervisor. I'm transferred to Sales so they can help. They can't help, so I'm transferred to Care, but instead, the call drops. I have to call back. So now I'm waiting on hold, having spent hours trying to resolve this. That easy promise, no issues, just set it up? That's not true. And when you run into issues, good luck finding help.

In addition to AWFUL network coverage - TMobile has this really cool (NOT) benefit of 6 months of Apple TV -- but when you go to cancel it ... it is like going on an extended treasure hunt ... so screw 'em ...

I’m giving T-Mobile 0 stars for what has been the most appalling customer service experience I’ve ever had.

I recently signed up for new service and ordered three iPhone 16e devices under contract #110200713297. Not only did T-Mobile completely mishandle my order—they sent the phones to an address I haven’t lived at in over five years—but they also sent the wrong phones entirely. I received two Samsung phones and an older model iPhone, despite my order form clearly stating I was to receive three iPhone 16e devices.

That outdated address is not linked to my current AT&T account, not tied to the card I paid with, and not something I ever provided during this transaction. Yet, T-Mobile shipped my brand new devices there without verifying anything.

When I called customer service, I spoke with a supervisor named Mark (Operator ID: 625715) who admitted it was their fault, but told me my options were:
	•	Wait for the phones to be misdelivered and hope they were returned,
	•	Or pay $14.99 out of my own pocket to reroute the package through UPS—money I shouldn’t have to spend to fix their mistake.

Then the worst happened: on July 26 at 1:55 PM, someone impersonating me signed for the package at the incorrect address. I filed a police report immediately (#T25009110) and contacted T-Mobile again. I was told by Dem (Operator ID: 732602) that I would have to wait until Monday for someone to follow up. Apparently, theft and fraud aren’t urgent enough to warrant weekend support.

Let me recap what T-Mobile got wrong:
	•	Sent the wrong devices
	•	To the wrong address
	•	Allowed a fraudulent signature to accept them
	•	Refused to take action until the next business day
	•	Told me I would need to repay the $117 in taxes if I wanted to start a new order in person

I’ve also reached out to UPS, the Orange County Sheriff’s Office, and Fox 35 News because this situation has gone beyond poor service—this is gross negligence and a complete lack of accountability.

If this is how T-Mobile treats new customers, I can only imagine how they treat loyal ones. If you value your personal information, your money, or basic customer support—do not trust T-Mobile.

7 years with TMobile, only to face this disgraceful scam. Your support is absolutely irresponsible, with no access to managers. Just agents endlessly bouncing me around, making me fill out the same form repeatedly. Two days wasted, zero solution. Unacceptable.

T-Mobile is the worst business. I have ever had to deal with. I have to call every month and get my service turned back on and paid the $60 charge every month. Reinstate, because I don't get paid till the 1st of the month, I tell them that, and they kill me, I have to paint on the 25th I tell them this every single month, they say they correct it, but yet Here I am, once again paying this $60 to reinstate. Also, they lie, they tell me, they take care of something. And then they don't do what I call back in and they say, oh well, that's there's no notes of that. Well, if they would pull the call like, they say they record it, maybe they'll see that their employees don't know what the hell they're talking about. Stay away from this company. Run, go to Verizon or a booster somewhere anywhere but T-Mobile? Because they all lie and all they do is read the script. They don't hear you listen to you or care what you have to say, or think.
